# google_migration_center_assets_export_job

AssetsExportJob represents a batch job that exports Migration Center assets to external destinations such as Cloud Storage.



<div class = "oics-button" style="float: right; margin: 0 0 -15px">
  <a href="https://console.cloud.google.com/cloudshell/open?cloudshell_git_repo=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fterraform-google-modules%2Fdocs-examples.git&cloudshell_image=gcr.io%2Fcloudshell-images%2Fcloudshell%3Alatest&cloudshell_print=.%2Fmotd&cloudshell_tutorial=.%2Ftutorial.md&cloudshell_working_dir=migration_center_assets_export_job_basic&open_in_editor=main.tf" target="_blank">
    <img alt="Open in Cloud Shell" src="//gstatic.com/cloudssh/images/open-btn.svg" style="max-height: 44px; margin: 32px auto; max-width: 100%;">
  </a>
</div>
## Example Usage - Migration Center Assets Export Job Basic


```hcl
resource "google_migration_center_assets_export_job" "default" {
  location             = "us-central1"
  assets_export_job_id = "assets-export-job-test"
  performance_data {
    max_days = 30
  }
  show_hidden = true
  signed_uri_destination {
    file_format = "CSV"
  }
  labels = {
    key = "value"
  }
}
```

## Argument Reference

The following arguments are supported:


* `location` -
  (Required)
  Resource ID segment making up resource `name`. It identifies the resource within its parent collection as described in https://google.aip.dev/122.

* `assets_export_job_id` -
  (Required)
  The ID to use for the asset export job.


* `condition` -
  (Optional)
  Conditions for selecting assets to export.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_condition).

* `labels` -
  (Optional)
  Labels as key value pairs.
  Labels must meet the following constraints:
  * Keys and values can contain only lowercase letters, numeric characters,
  underscores, and dashes.
  * All characters must use UTF-8 encoding, and international characters are
  allowed.
  * Keys must start with a lowercase letter or international character.
  * Each resource is limited to a maximum of 64 labels.
  Both keys and values are additionally constrained to be <= 128 bytes.
  **Note**: This field is non-authoritative, and will only manage the labels present in your configuration.
  Please refer to the field `effective_labels` for all of the labels present on the resource.

* `performance_data` -
  (Optional)
  Configuration for performance data exports.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_performance_data).

* `show_hidden` -
  (Optional)
  When this value is set to 'true' the response will include all assets,
  including those that are hidden.

* `signed_uri_destination` -
  (Optional)
  Signed URI destination configuration.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_signed_uri_destination).

* `project` - (Optional) The ID of the project in which the resource belongs.
    If it is not provided, the provider project is used.

* `deletion_policy` - (Optional) Whether Terraform will be prevented from destroying the resource. Defaults to DELETE.
	When a 'terraform destroy' or 'terraform apply' would delete the resource,
	the command will fail if this field is set to "PREVENT" in Terraform state.
	When set to "ABANDON", the command will remove the resource from Terraform
	management without updating or deleting the resource in the API.
	When set to "DELETE", deleting the resource is allowed.


<a name="nested_condition"></a>The `condition` block supports:

* `filter` -
  (Optional)
  Assets filter, supports the same syntax as asset listing.

<a name="nested_performance_data"></a>The `performance_data` block supports:

* `max_days` -
  (Optional)
  When this value is set to a positive integer,
  performance data will be returned for the most recent days
  for which data is available. When this value is unset (or set to zero),
  all available data is returned.
  The maximum value is 420; values above 420 will be coerced to 420.
  If unset (0 value) a default value of 40 will be used.

<a name="nested_signed_uri_destination"></a>The `signed_uri_destination` block supports:

* `file_format` -
  (Required)
  The file format to export.
  Possible values:
  CSV
  XLSX

## Attributes Reference

In addition to the arguments listed above, the following computed attributes are exported:

* `id` - an identifier for the resource with format `projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/assetsExportJobs/{{assets_export_job_id}}`

* `create_time` -
  Resource creation time.

* `inventory` -
  Configuration for asset inventory details exports.

* `name` -
  Identifier. Resource name.

* `network_dependencies` -
  Configuration for network dependencies exports.

* `recent_executions` -
  Recent non expired executions of the job.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_recent_executions).

* `update_time` -
  Resource update time.

* `terraform_labels` -
  The combination of labels configured directly on the resource
   and default labels configured on the provider.

* `effective_labels` -
  All of labels (key/value pairs) present on the resource in GCP, including the labels configured through Terraform, other clients and services.


<a name="nested_recent_executions"></a>The `recent_executions` block contains:

* `end_time` -
  (Output)
  Completion time of the export.

* `execution_id` -
  (Output)
  Globally unique identifier of the execution.

* `expire_time` -
  (Output)
  Expiration time for the export and artifacts.

* `requested_asset_count` -
  (Output)
  Number of assets requested for export after resolving the requested
  filters.

* `result` -
  (Output)
  Contains the result of the assets export.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_recent_executions_result).

* `start_time` -
  (Output)
  Execution timestamp.


<a name="nested_recent_executions_result"></a>The `result` block contains:

* `error` -
  (Output)
  The `Status` type defines a logical error model that is suitable for
  different programming environments, including REST APIs and RPC APIs. It is
  used by [gRPC](https://github.com/grpc). Each `Status` message contains
  three pieces of data: error code, error message, and error details.
  You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the
  [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_recent_executions_result_error).

* `output_files` -
  (Output)
  Contains a list of output files.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_recent_executions_result_output_files).

* `signed_uris` -
  (Output)
  Contains a list of Signed URIs.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_recent_executions_result_signed_uris).


<a name="nested_recent_executions_result_error"></a>The `error` block contains:

* `code` -
  (Output)
  The status code, which should be an enum value of google.rpc.Code.

* `details` -
  (Output)
  A list of messages that carry the error details.  There is a common set of
  message types for APIs to use.

* `message` -
  (Output)
  A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any
  user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the
  google.rpc.Status.details field, or localized by the client.

<a name="nested_recent_executions_result_output_files"></a>The `output_files` block contains:

* `entries` -
  (Output)
  List of output files.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_recent_executions_result_output_files_entries).


<a name="nested_recent_executions_result_output_files_entries"></a>The `entries` block contains:

* `csv_output_file` -
  (Output)
  Contains a single output file of type CSV.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_recent_executions_result_output_files_entries_csv_output_file).

* `file_size_bytes` -
  (Output)
  File size in bytes.

* `xlsx_output_file` -
  (Output)
  Contains a single output file of type XLSX.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_recent_executions_result_output_files_entries_xlsx_output_file).


<a name="nested_recent_executions_result_output_files_entries_csv_output_file"></a>The `csv_output_file` block contains:

* `columns_count` -
  (Output)
  Number of columns in the file.

* `row_count` -
  (Output)
  Number of rows in the file.

* `signed_uri` -
  (Output)
  Contains a signed URI.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_recent_executions_result_output_files_entries_csv_output_file_signed_uri).


<a name="nested_recent_executions_result_output_files_entries_csv_output_file_signed_uri"></a>The `signed_uri` block contains:

* `file` -
  (Output)
  Name of the file the Signed URI references.

* `uri` -
  (Output)
  Download URI for the file.

<a name="nested_recent_executions_result_output_files_entries_xlsx_output_file"></a>The `xlsx_output_file` block contains:

* `signed_uri` -
  (Output)
  Contains a signed URI.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_recent_executions_result_output_files_entries_xlsx_output_file_signed_uri).


<a name="nested_recent_executions_result_output_files_entries_xlsx_output_file_signed_uri"></a>The `signed_uri` block contains:

* `file` -
  (Output)
  Name of the file the Signed URI references.

* `uri` -
  (Output)
  Download URI for the file.

<a name="nested_recent_executions_result_signed_uris"></a>The `signed_uris` block contains:

* `signed_uris` -
  (Output)
  List of signed URIs.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_recent_executions_result_signed_uris_signed_uris).


<a name="nested_recent_executions_result_signed_uris_signed_uris"></a>The `signed_uris` block contains:

* `file` -
  (Output)
  Name of the file the Signed URI references.

* `uri` -
  (Output)
  Download URI for the file.

## Timeouts

This resource provides the following
[Timeouts](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/plugin/sdkv2/resources/retries-and-customizable-timeouts) configuration options:

- `create` - Default is 20 minutes.
- `update` - Default is 20 minutes.
- `delete` - Default is 20 minutes.

## Import


AssetsExportJob can be imported using any of these accepted formats:

* `projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/assetsExportJobs/{{assets_export_job_id}}`
* `{{project}}/{{location}}/{{assets_export_job_id}}`
* `{{location}}/{{assets_export_job_id}}`

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, use an [`identity` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/identities) to import AssetsExportJob using identity values. For example:

```tf
import {
  identity = {
    location = "<-required value->"
    assetsExportJobId = "<-required value->"
    project = "<-optional value->"
  }
  to = google_migration_center_assets_export_job.default
}
```

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, use an [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) to import AssetsExportJob using one of the formats above. For example:

```tf
import {
  id = "projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/assetsExportJobs/{{assets_export_job_id}}"
  to = google_migration_center_assets_export_job.default
}
```

When using the [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import), AssetsExportJob can be imported using one of the formats above. For example:

```
$ terraform import google_migration_center_assets_export_job.default projects/{{project}}/locations/{{location}}/assetsExportJobs/{{assets_export_job_id}}
$ terraform import google_migration_center_assets_export_job.default {{project}}/{{location}}/{{assets_export_job_id}}
$ terraform import google_migration_center_assets_export_job.default {{location}}/{{assets_export_job_id}}
```

## User Project Overrides

This resource supports [User Project Overrides](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/google/latest/docs/guides/provider_reference#user_project_override).
